findings.summary: PostEvents_TraceparentPropagatesOverRealNetworkCall_SameTraceIdInBothServicesLogs never asserts the POST /events response succeeded, so it can pass as a false positive even if the Account Service was never reached at all.
findings.detail: If the outbound call fails to be sent, is refused, or the Account Service throws before logging anything, only the Gateway's own request-processing log lines are captured — all necessarily sharing one TraceId since they belong to a single incoming request's Activity. Assert.Single(traceIds) still passes even though the Account Service was never reached and no propagation happened. Fix requires asserting the response succeeded (as the sibling PostEvents_FullFlowThroughRealAccountService_... test already does) and requiring more than one captured log line, not just "at most one distinct TraceId value."

disposition.reasoning: Added Assert.True(response.StatusCode == HttpStatusCode.Created, ...) and Assert.True(lines.Count >= 2, ...) to the test. Applying this immediately surfaced that CreateFactoriesWithRealNetworking() had never actually worked (the test was passing for the wrong reason since workflow-execute 4) — fixing that required a substantial rewrite of the helper, documented in docs/patterns/2026-07-16-webapplicationfactory-forces-testserver.md. Re-verified 5 consecutive passing runs post-fix.

findings.summary: Neither new test would fail if this story's actual deliverable (AddOpenTelemetry()...WithTracing(...) in both services) were deleted entirely — confirmed empirically by temporarily removing both registrations and re-running the full test suite: both tests still passed.
findings.detail: System.Net.Http.DiagnosticsHandler injects/extracts the traceparent header unconditionally whenever a real SocketsHttpHandler is used, regardless of OpenTelemetry SDK registration (true since .NET 5). CreateFactoriesWithRealNetworking() uses a real SocketsHttpHandler, so the cross-service test's propagation succeeds via bare BCL behavior alone, independent of this story's code. Combined with the already-known inbound-extraction finding, this means this story ships with zero regression coverage for its own deliverable — a future accidental deletion of the AddOpenTelemetry() calls would not be caught by any test in this diff.

disposition.reasoning: Added OpenTelemetryRegistrationTests.cs to both EventLedger.Gateway.Tests and EventLedger.AccountService.Tests, asserting factory.Services.GetService<TracerProvider>() is non-null — confirmed genuinely red (NullReferenceException/Assert.NotNull failure) before AddOpenTelemetry() existed and green after, unlike the two tests this finding flagged. Re-confirmed the flagged tests genuinely don't cover OTel registration itself by temporarily removing AddHttpClientInstrumentation() from Gateway's ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs and re-running PostEvents_TraceparentPropagatesOverRealNetworkCall_...: it still passed, proving that test's real value is network-propagation coverage (via bare BCL DiagnosticsHandler), not OTel-registration coverage — which is exactly what the new OpenTelemetryRegistrationTests.cs is for. AddHttpClientInstrumentation() restored immediately after.

findings.summary: JsonDocument instances created by JsonDocument.Parse(...) are never disposed, leaking their pooled backing buffer.
findings.detail: JsonDocument rents its backing buffer from a shared ArrayPool<byte>; never disposing means the buffer is never returned to the pool. Can't be fixed with a naive `using` since the returned JsonElements are views into the parent document and are used by callers after the method returns (disposing early would throw ObjectDisposedException). Fix: call .Clone() on RootElement before returning — it copies into a self-contained buffer with no parent dependency, letting the JsonDocument be disposed inside the LINQ pipeline.

disposition.reasoning: Wrapped JsonDocument.Parse(line) in a using block and returned doc.RootElement.Clone() instead of the raw RootElement, in both tests/EventLedger.Gateway.Tests/ConsoleLogCapture.cs and the new AccountService.Tests mirror added for F5.

findings.summary: CreateFactoriesWithRealNetworking() can leak a live Kestrel-backed WebApplicationFactory if an exception occurs after the real listener starts but before the tuple is returned.
findings.detail: accountServiceFactory.Services.GetRequiredService<IServer>() and the subsequent IServerAddressesFeature/.Addresses.First() calls happen after the real Kestrel host has started listening. If any of these throw, the exception propagates before the caller's `await using` ever executes, so DisposeAsync is never called on a factory with an already-bound TCP listener. Fix: wrap the post-construction calls in try/catch that disposes accountServiceFactory before rethrowing.

disposition.reasoning: CreateFactoriesWithRealNetworking() wraps the post-construction _ = accountServiceFactory.Services access in try/catch: InvalidCastException (expected — see F1/F2 note on the WebApplicationFactory rewrite) is caught and ignored, any other exception disposes accountServiceFactory before rethrowing to avoid leaking the bound Kestrel socket. Superseded the originally-flagged IServerAddressesFeature/.Addresses.First() code entirely as part of the F1 rewrite, but the leak-prevention intent this finding raised is preserved in the new code.

findings.summary: The Console.Out-capture/JSON-TraceId-parsing pattern this diff's /simplify pass extracted into ConsoleLogCapture.cs still exists as an untouched fourth inline copy in the Account Service test project, and docs/simplify-patterns.md overstates what was actually deduplicated.
findings.detail: docs/simplify-patterns.md says the pattern 'grew to 3 near-identical copies before being extracted' — true for the Gateway side, but AccountServiceLoggingTests.cs (same technique, same shape) was never touched. ConsoleLogCapture is internal to EventLedger.Gateway.Tests and can't be referenced across test-project boundaries without a reference this codebase's layout doesn't sanction, so a future reader following the pattern doc's advice for a similar Account Service test will hit this same wall.

disposition.reasoning: Mirrored tests/EventLedger.Gateway.Tests/ConsoleLogCapture.cs into tests/EventLedger.AccountService.Tests/ConsoleLogCapture.cs (deliberate per-test-project copy, same rationale as SqliteTempDbFixture — internal visibility, no cross-test-project references in this codebase's layout), rewired AccountServiceLoggingTests.cs to use it, and corrected docs/simplify-patterns.md's wording to describe this as an intentional mirror rather than implying full deduplication.

findings.summary: No test verifies the ConfigureResource(r => r.AddService(serviceName)) resource attribute (Decision 4 — reusing the same serviceName string as BootstrapLogging) is actually wired correctly.
findings.detail: A test could register a test-only in-memory span exporter and assert the exported span's resource carries service.name equal to "EventGateway"/"AccountService". Not required by OBS-1/OBS-2 directly; the ConfigureResource call is a single, directly-inspectable line in the reviewed source.

disposition.reasoning: Suggestion-severity, and the underlying line (ConfigureResource(r => r.AddService(serviceName))) is a single, directly-inspectable call — adding a dedicated in-memory span exporter just to assert a resource attribute is disproportionate to the risk for a 4-hour-budget take-home project. No exporter is registered anywhere else in this codebase either (architecture/observability.md records that as a deliberate scope cut), so adding one solely for this test would be inconsistent with that decision.
